---
title: "Discussion & R^2"
crossref:
  lst-title: "🎯 Checkpoint 6."
  lst-labels: alpha a
format: live-html
sidebar: lesson-3
---

### Making Predictions
:::{.gray-container}
**Materials Needed:**
- [RGB Applet V2](https://appalachianaicorps.org/modules/water-quality/RGB-Applet-2.html)
- Three Additional "Nitrate Test Pad" Cards
- Pencil
:::

:::{.dkorange-container}
Re-enter your color values and nitrate concentrations from the previous activity.

**IMPORTANT:** The values must be in the exact same order as before.

Click the ▶️ Run Code button to re-load your data.
:::

```{pyodide}
#| persist: true
#| completion: false
import numpy as np

color_data = [_, _, _, _, _, _, _, _]

nitrate_ppm = [_, _, _, _, _, _, _, _]

# Do not change the code below ------------------------------------
color_data = np.array(color_data)
nitrate_ppm = np.array(nitrate_ppm)
slope, intercept = np.polyfit(color_data, nitrate_ppm, 1)
print(f"Slope: {slope:.4f}, Intercept: {intercept:.4f}")
```

Now that we have our regression model, we can use it to **predict** nitrate concentrations for new color values — even ones we didn't measure!

:::{.dkorange-container}
Click the ▶️ Run Code button to create an interactive plot. Then **drag the slider** to move along the regression line and read off the predicted nitrate for any color value.
:::

```{pyodide}
import micropip
await micropip.install("plotly")
import plotly.graph_objects as go
from IPython.display import display, HTML
import numpy as np

x_min = float(min(color_data))
x_max = float(max(color_data))
x_line = np.linspace(x_min, x_max, 200)
y_line = slope * x_line + intercept

n_steps = 60
x_steps = np.linspace(x_min, x_max, n_steps)
y_steps = slope * x_steps + intercept

frames = [
    go.Frame(
        data=[go.Scatter(
            x=[float(xs)], y=[float(ys)],
            mode='markers+text',
            marker=dict(color='dodgerblue', size=14, line=dict(color='white', width=2)),
            text=[f'({xs:.1f}, {ys:.2f} ppm)'],
            textposition='top right',
            textfont=dict(size=13, color='navy')
        )],
        traces=[2],
        name=str(i)
    )
    for i, (xs, ys) in enumerate(zip(x_steps, y_steps))
]

x0, y0 = float(x_steps[0]), float(y_steps[0])

fig = go.Figure(
    data=[
        go.Scatter(
            x=list(color_data), y=list(nitrate_ppm),
            mode='markers', name='Your Data',
            marker=dict(color='black', size=9)
        ),
        go.Scatter(
            x=list(x_line), y=list(y_line),
            mode='lines', name='Regression Line',
            line=dict(color='tomato', width=2.5),
            hoverinfo='skip'
        ),
        go.Scatter(
            x=[x0], y=[y0],
            mode='markers+text', name='Prediction',
            marker=dict(color='dodgerblue', size=14, line=dict(color='white', width=2)),
            text=[f'({x0:.1f}, {y0:.2f} ppm)'],
            textposition='top right',
            textfont=dict(size=13, color='navy')
        )
    ],
    frames=frames,
    layout=go.Layout(
        title='Drag the slider to predict nitrate from any color value!',
        xaxis_title='Color Value',
        yaxis_title='Nitrate (ppm)',
        hovermode='closest',
        height=500,
        sliders=[{
            'active': 0,
            'steps': [
                {
                    'args': [[str(i)], {'frame': {'duration': 0, 'redraw': True}, 'mode': 'immediate'}],
                    'label': f'{xs:.0f}',
                    'method': 'animate'
                }
                for i, xs in enumerate(x_steps)
            ],
            'x': 0.05,
            'y': 0,
            'len': 0.9,
            'currentvalue': {
                'prefix': 'Color value: ',
                'visible': True,
                'xanchor': 'center',
                'font': {'size': 15, 'color': 'navy'}
            },
            'transition': {'duration': 0},
            'pad': {'t': 50}
        }]
    )
)

display(HTML(fig.to_html(include_plotlyjs='cdn', full_html=False)))
```
